rationale: The cited paper is used for more than a passing background mention: the citing paper repeatedly relies on it to justify how review-text positivity is operationalized. Specifically, Woolley and Sharif adopt the Berger and Milkman-style LIWC relative positivity measure (percent positive minus percent negative words) as a core dependent variable across studies. The citation also supports framing claims that positive content is more likely to be transmitted, linking their results to WOM diffusion implications.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Berger and Milkman (2012)'s content-valence lens and text-based positivity operationalization to a new problem: how incentives change the positivity of product reviews. It uses that prior framework as methodological and interpretive support rather than directly extending its arousal mechanism.
